title: Properly Proximal von Neumann Algebras
why_relevant: This is the foundational paper defining proper proximality for finite von Neumann algebras via the operator-system compactification S(M). It also proves key examples and criteria, including free-product, crossed-product, relative-boundary, tensor-product, and malleable-deformation tools that are directly relevant to graph-product von Neumann algebras.

title: Properly proximal groups and their von Neumann algebras
why_relevant: Introduces proper proximality for groups and the small-at-infinity boundary viewpoint. The group analogue for irreducible graph products is a central model for the desired von Neumann algebra statement, and the paper supplies the original central-state obstruction philosophy.

title: Proper proximality for various families of groups
why_relevant: Contains the complete classification of proper proximality for graph products of nontrivial groups. Its graph-product and relative-to-full proper-proximality upgrade arguments are likely the closest group-theoretic template for proving the stated von Neumann algebra result.

title: Graph products of operator algebras
why_relevant: Introduces graph products of C*-algebras and von Neumann algebras, including the Fock-space/reduced-word construction, conditional expectations, and basic permanence properties. These definitions and reduced-word calculations are indispensable for any proof about graph-product von Neumann algebras.

title: On the structure of graph product von Neumann algebras
why_relevant: Gives a detailed structural theory for graph-product von Neumann algebras, including bimodule decompositions for induced-subgraph subalgebras and classifications of relative amenability, fullness, diffuseness, and factoriality. These results are likely essential for using irreducibility of the graph and the trace-zero-unitary hypothesis to rule out boundary-central states.

title: Rigid Graph Products
why_relevant: Develops rigidity results for von Neumann algebraic graph products and proves technical theorems on relative amenability and embeddings of quasi-normalizers. Those tools are relevant for controlling subgraph subalgebras and excluding tensor/join-type obstructions in irreducible graph products.

title: On the structure of relatively biexact group von Neumann algebras
why_relevant: Develops von Neumann algebra-level techniques for upgrading relative proper proximality to full proper proximality using bidual computations. This is directly relevant if the graph-product proof first establishes proper proximality relative to link/star subalgebras and then uses irreducibility to upgrade.

title: Biexact von Neumann algebras
why_relevant: Introduces biexactness for general von Neumann algebras and develops the associated small-at-infinity and M-nuclearity technology. Even if the target theorem is about proper proximality rather than biexactness, these boundary and compactness techniques are part of the same operator-algebraic toolbox.

title: On Relative Biexactness of Amalgamated Free Product von Neumann Algebras
why_relevant: Graph-product von Neumann algebras admit natural decompositions as amalgamated free products over link subalgebras. This paper supplies recent relative biexactness and Toeplitz-Pimsner techniques for amalgamated free products, which may be useful for a relative-boundary approach to the problem.

title: Proper proximality in non-positive curvature
why_relevant: Provides geometric criteria for proper proximality for groups acting on CAT(0) spaces and CAT(0) cube complexes. It is useful for understanding the group-theoretic graph-product analogue and for importing boundary/proximality ideas from non-positive curvature.

title: Cartan subalgebras in von Neumann algebras associated with graph product groups
why_relevant: Contains operator-algebraic techniques specialized to von Neumann algebras of graph-product groups, including parabolic-subgroup, relative-amenability, and intertwining arguments. These are relevant background for translating graph-product combinatorics into von Neumann algebraic structural statements.
